feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.content: I was playing Doom Eternal the other day, and it suddenly dawned on me that sex with men is more pleasurable because you can see his penis is erect, and you know he is genuinely aroused by you. Sex with women is a relatively hollow experience, because she could just be faking it, and she probably is.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Drug Use In The Music Industry
entry.content: There's a big misconception in the music community that's been peddled by normies both on social media and in real life regarding drugs in the music community. Most of them seem to be of the opinion that musicians do drugs to "inspire" them to create ground breaking and era defining art/music, but the fact of the matter is, the majority of musicians do drugs to wind down, combat stage fright, to help them focus, and due to the rigorous schedule that comes with being a famous musician. The majori

The Merrymen were one of the biggest bands from the 1960's calypso craze, but are somewhat forgotten today: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=foN GipJDI7s >BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (CMC) — Prime Minister Mia Mottley on Friday led tributes to Sir Charles Emile Straker, the legendary Barbadian entertainer and lead vocalist of The Merrymen, who died earlier in the day at the age of 90. >Through songs such as Beautiful Barbados, Nut Seller, Big Bamboo, S

entry.title: /news/ News: Trump shows off new Air Force One, thanks Qatar
entry.content: https://nypost.com/2026/06/19/us-ne ws/aging-air-force-one-jet-gets-mot hballed-as-trump-preps-qatars-luxur y-747-for-flight/ WASHINGTON — President Trump unveiled the new Air Force One Friday — thanking Qatar for donating “the world’s most luxurious plane,” which he said would allow the United States to be “represented properly” as he travels the globe. “It was built at a level that will probably never be seen again,” Trump told onlookers in a new hangar built at Joint Base Andrews for the larg

entry.title: /g/ Technology: what is the technological problem with youtube being unable to recommend new videos?
entry.content: why is that such an impossibility for it not to just reshuffle the existing videos? why must my homepage be refreshed 7+ times just for *one* new video to pop up? Is this some kind of unsolved problem in computer science or what? i remember back in the day youtube felt like an endless stream of new videos. What the fuck happened?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: WSL has solved linux
entry.content: >more performant than native installation >no battery life, driver or sleep mode issues >can play games on the side >ms office, adobe photoshop etc productivity apps just work >mfw wsl was the best loonix distro all along

entry.title: /o/ Auto: mazda2
entry.content: there isnt a better supermini >Very fun and engaging driving dynamics (i would say it's as good as the fiesta (though the fiesta has better more feelsome steering) >Rigid chasis, barely any body roll, great sharp turn in and tones of grip, no oversteer >Reliable, simple, fuel efficient NA engine (Skyactiv) with a timing chain and no bullshit turbos or wet belts (this engine can easily do 150k miles) >Nice snickity satisfying gearbox >Great driving position >Nice interiors >Very stable on the mot

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Games where you are up against serial killers
entry.content: I finished Supermassive games "The Devil in Me" a couple of hours ago. A choose your own adventure style game set on a tiny island where a HH Holmes fanboy has built a copy of his murder mansion and invited a true crime documentary crew to be his next victims. It made me think about how few games actually have you up against horrific human opponents like this. Zombies? sure, Werewolves? now and then, Vampires? boy you better to believe how many licks this hard drive can contain via the medium of

entry.title: /g/ Technology: 22,79,743 students in India need your help today!
entry.content: Hello 4chan, this is my first time here. Every year in India more than 2 million students appear for NEET (medical university entrance examination). Due to the incompetence of the government, every year the question paper is leaked and rich people pay around 15k USD to buy it and get the top ranks kicking out students who worked hard for those seats. The paper was leaked on such a large scale this year, that the exam will be re-conducted on 21st June 2026. But yet again, the leak mafia has tried

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Ok we get it, vim keybindings are cool and all. But why would you use an ancient…
entry.content: Ok we get it, vim keybindings are cool and all. But why would you use an ancient, deprecated text editor that lacks all the modern features every employed software developer needs? You do realize these keybindings and modal editing exist as plugins for every IDE worth using? Time to stop being a trooner and start using a white mans development enviroinment. This is what peak performance looks like.
